blocks.body: Present Simple: Forms and Use — V1, he/she/it + s/es, habits, facts, and repeated actions.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"In Present Simple, we use V1 with I / you / we / they and add -s / -es with he / she / it.","rules":[{"label":"I / You / We / They + V1","color":"emerald","text":"Use the base form of the verb with these subjects.","example":"I work. / They live here."},{"label":"He / She / It + V-s / -es","color":"blue","text":"With third person singular, usually add -s or -es.","example":"She works. / He watches TV."},{"label":"Main use","color":"amber","text":"Use this tense for habits, facts, and actions that happen regularly.","example":"We study every day. / Water boils at 100°C."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic formula","intro":"Remember the two main patterns.","items":[{"label":"I / You / We / They","title":"V1","subtitle":"I work. / You play. / We live here."},{"label":"He / She / It","title":"V-s / -es","subtitle":"He works. / She plays. / It rains a lot."},{"label":"-es forms","title":"goes / watches / finishes","subtitle":"After go, watch, finish, and similar verbs, we often add -es."},{"label":"Spelling","title":"study → studies","subtitle":"If the verb ends in consonant + y, write -ies."}]}

{"title":"2. When we use Present Simple","sections":[{"label":"Habits","color":"emerald","description":"Talk about things you do regularly every day, every week, or usually.","examples":[{"en":"I drink tea every morning.","ua":"I drink tea every morning."},{"en":"She walks to school.","ua":"She walks to school."}]},{"label":"Facts","color":"blue","description":"Use the tense for simple facts and general truths.","examples":[{"en":"The sun rises in the east.","ua":"The sun rises in the east."},{"en":"My brother lives in Kyiv.","ua":"My brother lives in Kyiv."}]},{"label":"Repeated actions","color":"amber","description":"Show actions that happen again and again as part of a schedule or routine.","examples":[{"en":"The bus leaves at eight.","ua":"The bus leaves at eight."},{"en":"We have English on Mondays.","ua":"We have English on Mondays."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Quick form comparison","intro":"See how the verb changes with different subjects.","rows":[{"en":"I work every day.","ua":"base form V1","note":"I + work"},{"en":"You work every day.","ua":"base form V1","note":"You + work"},{"en":"He works every day.","ua":"add -s","note":"He + works"},{"en":"She watches TV in the evening.","ua":"add -es","note":"She + watches"}],"warning":"Do not say he work or I goes."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Leaving out -s with he / she / it.","wrong":"❌ He play football after school.","right":"✅ He plays football after school."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Adding -s with I / you / we / they.","wrong":"❌ I goes to work at eight.","right":"✅ I go to work at eight."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Forgetting the spelling change with -y.","wrong":"❌ She study English on Mondays.","right":"✅ She studies English on Mondays."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["Use V1 with I / you / we / they.","With he / she / it, usually add -s / -es.","Present Simple is for habits, facts, and repeated actions.","After consonant + y, we often have -ies: study → studies.","A common mistake is he work instead of he works."]}
